tasks.title: Graceful shutdown handlers + env validation + connection pooling docs
tasks.description:
Add production-grade graceful shutdown to the API server, improve environment validation, and document connection pooling.

**Graceful Shutdown (`apps/api/src/index.ts`):**
The current entry point creates a PrismaClient, starts Express on `process.env.PORT`, and runs a 15-minute `setInterval` for due date reminders — but has NO shutdown handlers. On SIGTERM/SIGINT:
1. Store references to the server, prisma client, and interval handle.
2. Add `process.on('SIGTERM')` and `process.on('SIGINT')` handlers that: log "Shutting down gracefully...", stop accepting new connections (`server.close()`), clear the reminder interval, disconnect Prisma (`prisma.$disconnect()`), close any SSE connections (import `closeAllConnections` from `utils/sseManager.ts` if it exists, or iterate the manager), then `process.exit(0)`. Add a 10-second force-kill timeout (`setTimeout(() => process.exit(1), 10000).unref()`).
3. Add `process.on('unhandledRejection')` and `process.on('uncaughtException')` handlers that log the error and exit(1).
4. On startup, verify DB connectivity with `prisma.$queryRaw\`SELECT 1\`` — if it fails, log error and exit(1) instead of silently hanging.

**Environment Validation (`apps/api/src/app.ts` already has Zod validation at line 25-43):**
Don't touch app.ts (belongs to P2's file set). Instead, in `index.ts` after the env schema passes, add warnings (not errors) via `logger.warn()` if: `SMTP_HOST` is not set in production, `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` is not set, `ENCRYPTION_MASTER_KEY` is the default value.

**`.env.example` updates:**
Add comments documenting: `# Connection pooling: append ?connection_limit=20&pool_timeout=10 to DATABASE_URL for production`, `# SENTRY_DSN= (optional, for error tracking)`, and any other missing env vars found during implementation.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria:
- SIGTERM and SIGINT trigger graceful shutdown: Prisma disconnects, intervals clear, server stops
- 10-second force-kill timeout prevents zombie processes
- unhandledRejection and uncaughtException are caught and logged
- Startup verifies DB connectivity before accepting requests
- Production env warnings logged for missing SMTP_HOST, ANTHROPIC_API_KEY
- .env.example documents connection pooling params
- `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` pass
- No changes to apps/api/src/app.ts (that file belongs to P2)

tasks.title: React Error Boundary + static asset caching headers
tasks.description:
Add a global React Error Boundary so the app shows a friendly fallback UI instead of a blank white screen on component errors. Also add proper Cache-Control headers for static assets. **Error Boundary (`apps/web/src/components/ErrorBoundary.tsx` — new file):** Create a class component (React error boundaries must be class components) that: 1. Catches errors in `componentDidCatch(error, errorInfo)` and logs them to console.error. 2. Renders a fallback UI: centered card with a warning icon, heading "Something went wrong", the error message (in a collapsible `
` for debugging), and a "Reload page" button that calls `window.location.reload()`. 3. Also include a "Go home" link that navigates to `/`. 4. Style with Tailwind classes consistent with the app's design (slate colors, rounded cards). 5. Accept an optional `fallback` prop for custom fallback UI. **App.tsx integration (`apps/web/src/App.tsx`):** Wrap the entire `` block (currently lines ~28-65) in ``. Import from `../components/ErrorBoundary`. Also wrap with `
}>` for lazy-loaded route components. **Static asset caching (`apps/api/src/app.ts`):** The production static file serving is at line ~160-173 using `express.static()`. Update to: 1. For hashed assets in `dist/assets/` — serve with `Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable` (1 year, content-addressed). 2. For `index.html` and other root files — serve with `Cache-Control: no-cache` (always revalidate). Implement by adding a separate `express.static()` for the `assets` subdirectory with `maxAge: '1y'` and `immutable: true`, before the general static handler. The general handler should set `setHeaders: (res, path) => { if (path.endsWith('.html')) res.setHeader('Cache-Control', 'no-cache'); }`.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria:
- ErrorBoundary class component catches errors and shows fallback UI with reload/home buttons
- Error message shown in collapsible details for debugging
- App.tsx wraps Routes in ErrorBoundary and Suspense
- Hashed assets served with Cache-Control: public, max-age=31536000, immutable
- index.html served with Cache-Control: no-cache
- `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` pass
- `pnpm build` succeeds

tasks.title: Shared accessible Modal component + convert all 19 modals + ARIA labels
tasks.description:
Create a reusable accessible Modal wrapper component, then convert all 19 existing modal/dialog components to use it. Also add ARIA labels to icon-only buttons and live regions to toasts.

**Shared Modal (`apps/web/src/components/shared/Modal.tsx` — new file):**
Create a `<Modal>` component with props: `isOpen`, `onClose`, `title` (for aria-labelledby), `size?: 'sm' | 'md' | 'lg' | 'xl'`, `children`. Implementation:
1. `role="dialog"`, `aria-modal="true"`, `aria-labelledby` pointing to a generated ID on the title element.
2. **Focus trap:** On mount, find all focusable elements inside the modal. On Tab, cycle focus within the modal (last→first, Shift+Tab first→last). Save `document.activeElement` on open and restore focus on close.
3. **Escape to close:** `useEffect` with `keydown` listener for Escape key calling `onClose`.
4. **Backdrop click:** The overlay `div` calls `onClose` on click; the inner container stops propagation.
5. **Portal:** Render via `createPortal` to `document.body` to avoid z-index stacking issues.
6. **Animation:** Keep the existing `fixed inset-0 z-50` pattern with `bg-black/40` backdrop.
7. Size maps: sm=max-w-md, md=max-w-2xl, lg=max-w-4xl, xl=max-w-6xl.

Do NOT add any npm dependencies for focus trapping — implement it manually (query `[tabindex], a[href], button:not([disabled]), input:not([disabled]), select:not([disabled]), textarea:not([disabled])`).

**Convert all 19 modals:** Each modal currently uses a raw `<div className="fixed inset-0 z-50...">` wrapper. Replace with `<Modal isOpen={...} onClose={...} title="..." size="...">`. Remove the duplicated backdrop div, the manual z-50/fixed positioning, and any ad-hoc Escape key handlers. Keep the modal's inner content (header with title, body, footer) intact.

The 19 components: BatchCodeGenModal, BugReportModal, CSVImportModal, CloseSprintModal, CodePreviewModal, DriftAnalysisModal, GitHubRepoModal, GlobalSearchModal, KnowledgeBaseModal, MeetingNotesDialog, PRDBreakdownModal, ProjectChatPanel, ProjectSettingsModal, SprintCreateModal, SprintPlanModal, SprintReportPanel, SprintTransitionModal, TaskPlanApprovalDialog, KeyboardShortcutHelp.

Note: ProjectChatPanel and SprintReportPanel are side panels, not centered modals. The Modal component should support a `variant?: 'centered' | 'panel-right'` prop for these.

**ARIA labels on icon-only buttons:** Audit all components for buttons that only contain an icon (SVG or emoji like ✕, ↻, ✎). Add `aria-label` with a descriptive action. Key locations: close buttons on all modals (add to Modal component itself), AppLayout settings/notifications/search buttons, SearchInput clear button, ToastContainer close button, CodePreviewModal regenerate/feedback buttons, FilterBar delete-filter buttons.

**Live regions:** Add `role="status"` and `aria-live="polite"` to the ToastContainer wrapper. For error toasts, use `role="alert"` and `aria-live="assertive"`. Add `aria-hidden="true"` to decorative SVG icons in Icons.tsx.

**Color contrast:** In Icons.tsx, ensure all SVGs have `aria-hidden="true"` so screen readers skip decorative icons.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria:
- Shared Modal component has: focus trap, Escape to close, aria-modal, aria-labelledby, backdrop click, focus restoration
- All 19 modal/dialog components converted to use shared Modal
- No raw `fixed inset-0 z-50` modal wrappers remain outside Modal.tsx
- All icon-only buttons have aria-label attributes
- ToastContainer has aria-live region
- SVG icons in Icons.tsx have aria-hidden="true"
- `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` pass
- No new npm dependencies added

tasks.title: Skip-to-content, form labels, KanbanBoard keyboard navigation
tasks.description:
Add remaining WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility improvements: skip navigation link, form label associations, keyboard-accessible kanban board, and semantic improvements. **Skip-to-content link (`apps/web/src/pages/AppLayout.tsx`):** Add a visually-hidden skip link as the very first child of the layout: `Skip to main content`. Add `id="main-content"` to the `
` element. Also: 1. Add `aria-label` to the sidebar `
` element (e.g., `aria-label="Main navigation"`). 2. Add `aria-label` to icon-only buttons in the header bar: the settings gear (~line 135-142), notifications bell (~line 149-168), and quick-search button (~line 111-121). The notifications bell should also have an `aria-label` that includes the unread count (e.g., `aria-label="Notifications (3 unread)"` or just `aria-label="Notifications"` when 0). 3. Add `aria-live="polite"` to the notification count badge so screen readers announce updates. 4. The "Live updates active" indicator (~line 78-82) should have `aria-live="polite"` and `role="status"`. **KanbanBoard keyboard navigation (`apps/web/src/components/KanbanBoard.tsx`):** The current implementation uses HTML5 drag-and-drop with no keyboard alternative (WCAG 2.5.1 violation). Add keyboard support: 1. Make task cards focusable with `tabIndex={0}`. 2. On a focused card, pressing Enter or Space enters "move mode" — show a visual indicator (e.g., dashed border or highlight) and announce via an `aria-live` region. 3. In move mode, Left/Right arrow keys move the task to the adjacent column. Call `onColumnChange(taskId, newColumn)` — the same handler used by drag-and-drop. 4. Press Enter/Space again or Escape to exit move mode. 5. Add `aria-description="Press Enter to move this task between columns"` to each card. 6. Add `role="listbox"` to each column container and `role="option"` to each task card within it, with `aria-selected` for the currently-focused card. **Notification Center (`apps/web/src/components/NotificationCenter.tsx`):** If this component exists, ensure the notification dropdown has `role="menu"`, individual items have `role="menuitem"`, and the panel is keyboard navigable (arrow keys between items, Enter to activate, Escape to close). **Form label associations:** In AppLayout, ensure any `` elements have associated `
tasks.acceptanceCriteria:
- Skip-to-content link visible on focus, jumps to #main-content
- Sidebar nav has aria-label="Main navigation"
- All icon-only buttons in AppLayout header have aria-labels
- Notification badge count announced via aria-live
- KanbanBoard cards focusable with Tab
- Enter/Space on card enters move mode, arrow keys move between columns
- Escape exits move mode
- aria-live region announces card movement
- `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` pass

tasks.title: Webhook delivery tracking model + retry queue with exponential backoff
tasks.description:
Add persistent webhook delivery tracking and a retry queue with exponential backoff so failed webhooks are retried automatically and delivery history is visible.

**Prisma model (`apps/api/prisma/schema/webhook.prisma`):**
Add a `WebhookDelivery` model alongside the existing `WebhookEndpoint`:
```
model WebhookDelivery {
  id            String   @id @default(uuid())
  endpointId    String   @map("endpoint_id")
  event         String
  payload       String   // JSON string
  status        String   @default("pending") // pending, success, failed, retrying
  statusCode    Int?     @map("status_code")
  responseBody  String?  @map("response_body") // first 500 chars
  attemptCount  Int      @default(0) @map("attempt_count")
  nextRetryAt   DateTime? @map("next_retry_at")
  createdAt     DateTime @default(now()) @map("created_at")
  completedAt   DateTime? @map("completed_at")
  endpoint      WebhookEndpoint @relation(fields: [endpointId], references: [id], onDelete: Cascade)
  @@index([endpointId, status])
  @@index([status, nextRetryAt])
  @@map("webhook_deliveries")
}
```
Add `deliveries WebhookDelivery[]` relation to `WebhookEndpoint`. Run `npx prisma migrate dev --name add_webhook_deliveries`.

**Webhook dispatcher overhaul (`apps/api/src/utils/webhookDispatcher.ts`):**
Refactor `dispatchWebhooks()` to:
1. For each matching endpoint, create a `WebhookDelivery` record with status `pending`.
2. Attempt delivery (existing HTTP POST logic with HMAC signature).
3. On success (2xx): update delivery to `status: 'success'`, store `statusCode`, set `completedAt`.
4. On failure: update delivery to `status: 'retrying'`, increment `attemptCount`, calculate `nextRetryAt` using exponential backoff (delays: 5s, 30s, 2min, 10min, 1hr — 5 attempts max). After 5 attempts, set `status: 'failed'`.
5. Add a `processRetryQueue()` function that queries deliveries WHERE `status = 'retrying' AND nextRetryAt <= now()`, then re-attempts them. Call this on a 30-second `setInterval` in `index.ts` (store the handle for graceful shutdown).
6. Add a `replayDelivery(deliveryId)` function that resets a failed delivery to `pending` and re-dispatches.
7. Truncate `responseBody` to 500 chars to prevent DB bloat.

**Fix missing webhook dispatches:**
- `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/task.ts`: The `createComment` resolver (~line 321-382) dispatches SSE but does NOT call `dispatchWebhooks()` for `comment.created`. Add it.
- `apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/sprint.ts`: The `createSprint` resolver doesn't dispatch `sprint.created`. Add it.

**GraphQL types (`apps/api/src/graphql/typedefs/webhook.ts`):**
Add:
```graphql
type WebhookDelivery {
  id: ID!
  endpointId: ID!
  event: String!
  status: String!
  statusCode: Int
  attemptCount: Int!
  nextRetryAt: String
  createdAt: String!
  completedAt: String
}
```
Add query: `webhookDeliveries(endpointId: ID!, limit: Int): [WebhookDelivery!]!`
Add mutation: `replayWebhookDelivery(deliveryId: ID!): WebhookDelivery!`

**GraphQL resolvers (`apps/api/src/graphql/resolvers/webhook.ts`):**
Implement `webhookDeliveries` query (fetch last N deliveries for an endpoint, ordered by createdAt desc, default limit 50) and `replayWebhookDelivery` mutation (reset status to pending, clear nextRetryAt, re-dispatch).
tasks.acceptanceCriteria:
- WebhookDelivery model created with migration
- Every webhook dispatch creates a delivery record
- Failed deliveries retry with exponential backoff (5s, 30s, 2min, 10min, 1hr)
- After 5 failed attempts, delivery marked as 'failed'
- 30-second retry processor runs in background
- comment.created and sprint.created events now dispatch webhooks
- webhookDeliveries query returns delivery history
- replayWebhookDelivery mutation re-dispatches failed deliveries
- `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` pass
- Migration files committed

tasks.title: Webhook delivery dashboard UI + Slack command expansion + email HTML templates
tasks.description:
Add webhook delivery history UI, expand Slack slash commands, and create branded HTML email templates.

**Webhook Delivery Dashboard (`apps/web/src/components/WebhookSettings.tsx`):**
The existing component shows webhook endpoints with CRUD. Add a delivery history section:
1. When a webhook endpoint is expanded/selected, show a "Deliveries" tab alongside the existing config.
2. Query `webhookDeliveries(endpointId, limit: 20)` and display as a list: event name, status badge (green=success, yellow=retrying, red=failed, gray=pending), status code, attempt count, timestamp.
3. For failed deliveries, show a "Replay" button that calls `replayWebhookDelivery(deliveryId)` mutation.
4. Show summary stats at the top of each endpoint: success count, failure count, last delivery time.
5. Add the new queries/mutations to `apps/web/src/api/queries.ts`.

**Slack command expansion (`apps/api/src/slack/slackWebhookHandler.ts`):**
Currently only handles `create` and `help` commands. Add:
1. `/tasktoad list` — fetch the user's assigned tasks (up to 10) from the org's first project. Return a Block Kit formatted list with task title, status emoji (todo=⬜, in_progress=🔵, done=✅), and priority. If no tasks, show "No tasks assigned to you."
2. `/tasktoad status` — fetch project summary: total tasks, by-status breakdown, active sprint name. Format as a Block Kit section with fields.
3. Update `help` command to include the new commands.
4. In `slackClient.ts`, add `formatTaskList()` and `formatProjectStatus()` Block Kit builders.

**Email HTML templates (`apps/api/src/utils/email.ts`):**
The current `sendEmail()` sends plain text with optional HTML. Replace the three text-only template helpers with HTML templates:
1. `buildVerifyEmailHtml(url)` — branded header with app name, body explaining email verification, prominent CTA button linking to the URL, footer with support info.
2. `buildResetPasswordHtml(url)` — similar structure, urgency messaging, CTA button, expiry warning.
3. `buildInviteHtml(orgName, url)` — invitation context, org name mention, CTA button.
4. Use inline CSS (email clients don't support `<style>` blocks reliably). Base template: max-width 600px, light gray background, white card, slate-700 text, blue CTA buttons (#1e40af). Include both `html` and `text` versions in `sendEmail()` calls.
5. Add a `buildEmailBase(title, bodyHtml)` helper to reduce duplication across templates.
tasks.acceptanceCriteria:
- Webhook delivery history visible per endpoint with status badges
- Replay button works for failed deliveries
- `/tasktoad list` returns Block Kit formatted assigned tasks
- `/tasktoad status` returns project summary with task breakdown
- Help command shows all available commands
- Email verification, reset, and invite have branded HTML templates
- Plain text fallback still works
- `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm lint` pass
